title: Dupatya obtusifolia
taxonomy: Dupatya obtusifolia is classified within the family Celastraceae. This family consists of deciduous and evergreen trees and shrubs. The Celastraceae family is part of the larger order Celastrales. The Celastrales order is an ancient group of plants that dates back to the Cretaceous period. This classification places Dupatya obtusifolia among other plants that share similar characteristics, such as opposite leaves and small, often berry-like fruit.
uses: Dupatya obtusifolia is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its roots are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as for its antiseptic and antifungal properties. The plant's leaves and stems are also used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional medicine, Dupatya obtusifolia is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects in modern medicine.
